change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Correspondence with other bacteriologists, public health officials, publishers, commercial laboratories, and Cornell administrators, as well as reports, financial records, notes, and printed material, concerning his participation in the work of the Biological Stain Commission, National Research Council, American Public Health Association, and Society of American Bacteriologists, his scientific writings, and research projects.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Chief of Research Soil Bacteriology, N.Y.S. Agricultural Experiment Station at Geneva, 1920-1948.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Correspondence with other bacteriologists, public health officials, publishers, commercial laboratories, and Cornell administrators, as well as reports, financial records, notes, and printed material, all of which concern his participation in the work of the Biological Stain Commission, of which he was chairman, National Research Council, American Public Health Association, and Society of American Bacteriologists, his scientific writings, and research projects undertaken at Geneva and elsewhere; correspondents include Robert S. Breed, Arthur P. Hitchens, George J. Hucker, Floyd K. Richtmyer, William A. Stocking, and Selman A. Waksman. Also, copies of his publications, an autobiography (86 pp. typescript carbon), and "A Religious Scientist at the Turn of the Century" (123 pp. mimeo), his biographical essay on his father, Herbert William Conn, professor of bacteriology at Wesleyan University in Connecticut, in which is included a bibliography of his father's writings.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: H.J. Conn papers, #22-2-661. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

Public health.

did.unittitle: Loose H. K. Mulford Company correspondence
Ginn and Co. - Publishers; Silver Burdett & Co.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1924-1925: A-Z, Replies of CS Circ. Spring 1924, 1924-25 Form Letters

did.unittitle: Twelve National Aniline Co. (23) folders, labeled by month, Jan.-Dec.
National Aniline and Chem. Co., Jan.-March

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Replies to letter in JAMA, Portuguese letter about Commission, Spanish letter about Commission, Form letters

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Kabat 2; 3 Spiegelman; 4 Smith; 5 Braun; 6 Milzer; 7 Samanna; 8 Morgan; 1 Foster; 10 Lichstein; 9 Wissler.

did.unittitle: Advertising Rates- Pure Culture Study of Bacteria
Stock and Bond Record, Bank Books, etc.
Memoranda of Understanding, U.S.D.A. and C.S.B.S. and others
Biol. Stain Commission, Treasurers and Invest. Account
Rochester Electrotype and Engr., James Lennox
Stain Investigation Commission, Correspondence of Historical Interest
Historical letters, Com. On Technic-Stain Commission
Bundle of folders: Correspondence of 1920-21 on early stain investigation
" " " : Early Stain Investigations, 1921-23, which lead to formation of stain commission
Mimeographed sheets: A Religious Scientist at the Turn of the Century, Herbert William Conn of Wesleyan University, by Harold J. Conn, Geneva, N.Y.
